validator_independence.summary: Champion and validator independently measured BS=32 E2E improvement, converging to 1.41% vs 1.46% (5ms / 0.05% difference). Validator ran on same GPU 0 but independent scripts.

citation.claim: Champion vs validator E2E cross-check converges within 5ms at BS=32
citation.quoted_content:
| Metric | Champion (GPU 0) | Validator (GPU 0) | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| E2E avg | 10.141s | 10.136s | 5ms (0.05%) |
| E2E improvement | -145ms (1.41%) | -150ms (1.46%) | 0.05% |

validator_independence.summary: Validator independently tested all 5 shapes x BS=[1,8,32] (23/23 PASS), plus CUDA graph capture/replay and NaN/INF checks. Fused SiLU kernel confirmed more accurate than cuBLAS vs FP32 ground truth.

citation.claim: Validator independently passed 23/23 correctness tests across all shapes and batch sizes
citation.quoted_content:
### Validator Gate 5.1: Correctness -- PASS (23/23)

- All 5 shapes x BS=[1,8,32]: PASS
- down_proj BS=32: max_diff=2.0 (1 BF16 ULP at scale ~260; Triton more accurate vs FP32)
  - Triton vs FP32: 1.003, cuBLAS vs FP32: 1.634
- CUDA graph capture+replay: PASS

validator_independence.summary: Validator independently discovered warm regressions on down_proj (0.754x) and in_proj_qkvz (0.925x), triggering Kill Criterion #5 failure despite champion not reporting these specific numbers.

citation.claim: Validator independently found warm regressions on down_proj (0.754x) and in_proj (0.925x)

validator_independence.summary: Champion (GPU 0) and validator (GPU 1) independently benchmarked kernel speedup at BS=32 cold: 1.089x vs 1.072x (2% difference). Both confirmed >1.05x threshold.

citation.claim: Champion vs validator kernel benchmark cross-check within 2% at BS=32 cold
citation.quoted_content:
| Metric | Champion (GPU 0) | Validator (GPU 1) | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| Aggregate speedup | 1.089x | 1.072x | 2% |
| gate_up speedup | 1.117x | 1.112x | 0.5% |
| Saved/step | 969 us | 780 us | 20% |

validator_independence.summary: Validator independently tested correctness at BS=[1,8,32] with state max diff 3344x below kill criterion, plus adversarial near-zero decay test and CUDA graph verification.

citation.claim: Validator independently verified correctness with 1678x-3344x margin below kill criterion

validator_independence.summary: Validator independently profiled the Triton BV=32 baseline with NCU, revealing actual register count (168 regs/thread), occupancy (25% theoretical), and SOL bottleneck (DRAM >80%), which corrected the debate's stale estimates.

citation.claim: Validator NCU profiling revealed actual register count and occupancy, correcting debate estimates
citation.quoted_content:
| Metric | BS=1 | BS=8 | BS=32 |
|--------|------|------|-------|
| Registers/thread | 168 | 168 | 168 |
| Static SMEM/block | 0 B | 0 B | 0 B |
| Theoretical occupancy | 25% | 25% | 25% |
| Achieved occupancy | 2.1% | 13.9% | 22.3% |
| L1 hit rate | 49% | 25% | 1.2% |
| L2 hit rate | 55% | 51% | 51% |
| SOL bottleneck | DRAM >80% | DRAM >80% | DRAM >80% |

campaign_metrics.termination_reason: Campaign converged after 3 rounds. Both shipped optimizations failed their kill criteria but were shipped anyway: op007 at 1.41-1.46% (below 1.5% threshold), op006 at 0.66% (below 1.0% threshold). Combined ~2% at BS=32.
technology_classes: Triton GEMM (custom BF16 thin-tall GEMM for M=[2,32] decode)
Fused SiLU GEMM (gate+up projection with fused SiLU activation in Triton)
BV=8 recurrent tiling (smaller tiles for higher occupancy in GDN kernel)
Persistent CTA CUDA (attempted, failed: occupancy-limited at BV=128)
Split-K GEMM (attempted, validated warm fix but zero additional E2E)

citations.claim: Campaign completed 3 rounds, shipped op007 + op006, cumulative speedup 1.0x
citations.quoted_content:
"cumulative_e2e_speedup": 1.0,
    "current_round": 3,
    ..."shipped_optimizations": ["op007_selective_silu_gemm", "op006_bv8_recurrent"],
    "status": "campaign_complete"

citations.claim: Campaign summary with combined ~2% E2E at BS=32
citations.quoted_content: "summary": "CAMPAIGN COMPLETE. Shipped op007 (Triton GEMM, 1.45% E2E BS=32) + op006 (BV=8, 0.66% BS=32 / 1.5% BS=8). Combined: ~2% E2E at BS=32."

citations.claim: Hardware: 4x NVIDIA L40S, SM89, 46GB each
citations.quoted_content:
"clocks.max.sm": "2520",
        "compute_cap": "8.9",
        "driver_version": "580.126.16",
        "memory.total": "46068",
        "name": "NVIDIA L40S"

citations.claim: Target: Qwen/Qwen3.5-4B, BF16, TP=1, batch sizes [1, 8, 32]

anti_patterns.description: Op007 champion projected 2.0-2.5% E2E using hypothetical 0.556 translation factor, but actual E2E was 1.41-1.46%. Empirical translation factor of 0.33 matched op004's 0.37, contradicting the 0.556 hypothesis. Proposal made a ~1.5x overestimate.

citations.claim: Champion-1 projected 0.556 translation factor to reach 2.0% E2E from 723 us/step kernel savings
citations.quoted_content:
| Factor | ms saved | % E2E | Verdict |
|--------|---------|-------|----------|
| 0.37 (op004 calibration) | 137 | 1.3% | FAIL |
| 0.50 (conservative, no regressions) | 185 | 1.8% | FAIL |
| 0.556 (threshold) | 206 | 2.0% | PASS |
| 0.60 (expected) | 222 | 2.2% | PASS |

citations.claim: Actual validation showed translation factor of 0.33, far below 0.556 projection
citations.quoted_content:
| Factor | Value |
|---|---|
| f_GEMM (BS=32) | 79.6% |
| Cold kernel savings | 844.8 us/step |
| Amdahl's projection (512 steps) | 433 ms = 4.2% |
| Measured E2E (avg) | 145 ms = 1.4% |
| Translation factor | 0.33 |

citations.claim: Kill criterion #2 failed: E2E only 1.41-1.46%, below 1.5% threshold

anti_patterns.description: Both shipped optimizations (op007 and op006) failed their kill criteria. Op007 E2E 1.41-1.46% below 1.5% threshold, with warm regressions on 2 shapes. Op006 E2E 0.66% below 1.0% threshold, with kernel regression at BS=32. Campaign shipped anyway per 'compromise between risk and upside'.

citations.claim: Op007 fails 2 kill criteria: E2E below 1.5% and warm regressions on down_proj/in_proj
citations.quoted_content:
**Two kill criteria fail:**
- **Kill #2**: E2E improvement at BS=32 is 1.41-1.46%, below the 1.5% threshold (by 0.04-0.09%)
- **Kill #5**: down_proj (0.754x warm) and in_proj_qkvz (0.925x warm) regress in warm cache

citations.claim: Op006 fails 2 kill criteria: kernel regression at BS=32 and E2E below 1.0%
citations.quoted_content:
| 1 | Kernel speedup BS=32 cold | >=1.1x | 0.992x | **FAIL** |
| 2 | E2E improvement BS=32 | >=1.0% | 0.66% | **FAIL** |
| 3 | Correctness (FP32 state diff) | <1e-4 | 5.96e-08 | PASS |
| 4 | CUDA graph capture | Must succeed | Succeeded | PASS |

citations.claim: Op006 explicitly determined as FAIL, should NOT be shipped
citations.quoted_content:
**FAIL.** BV=8 does not deliver meaningful improvement at BS=32. The optimization should NOT be shipped.

The approach works at small batch sizes but the kill criteria correctly target BS=32 as the production-relevant workload.

anti_patterns.description: Round 1 spent resources on 2 BV=128 GDN proposals (op001_persistent_cta, op002_fused_gating_wide_bv) when the kernel was already at BW ceiling (1207 GB/s, 140% HBM peak). Both failed. Delegate correctly identified root cause only after resources were consumed.

citations.claim: Round 1 selected two BV=128 GDN candidates that both failed
citations.quoted_content: "selected_candidates": ["op001_persistent_cta", "op002_fused_gating_wide_bv"], "implementation_results": {"op001_persistent_cta": {"status": "FAILED", "reason": "Kernel 0.30x at BS=32. SMEM-based BV=128 halves blocks/SM."}, "op002_fused_gating_wide_bv": {"status": "FAILED"

citations.claim: Delegate identified root cause: kernel already at BW ceiling, BV=128 reduces data by only 1.16%
citations.quoted_content:
Both BV=128 approaches hit the same wall: **the kernel is already at its memory bandwidth ceiling**.
...BV=128 reduces total data per call by only **1.16%** (q/k deduplication: 1.52 MB out of 130.5 MB). The state is 98.3% of all traffic and scales identically with both BV settings.

citations.claim: Op001 persistent CTA kernel was 0.57x at BS=32 in best case (anti-optimal)

anti_patterns.description: Op008 Split-K fixed warm regressions on down_proj (0.76x -> 1.09x warm) but provided zero additional E2E because down_proj operates near-cold in production (gate_up_proj's 90 MB evicts L2 before down_proj runs). Warm benchmarks were structurally misleading for E2E prediction.

citations.claim: Split-K fixes warm regression but down_proj operates near-cold in production due to L2 eviction
citations.quoted_content:
In the Qwen3.5-4B decode pipeline, GEMMs execute in layer order:
1. `in_proj_qkvz` (60 MB) -> fills L2
2. GDN core -> partially evicts
3. `out_proj` (20 MB) -> partial L2
4. **`gate_up_proj` (90 MB) -> exceeds L2, evicts everything**
5. **`down_proj` (45 MB) -> follows 90 MB eviction -> near-cold**

citations.claim: Op008 E2E identical to op004: Split-K provides zero additional benefit
citations.quoted_content:
**Comparison with op004**: op004 P50 = 10.139s, op008 P50 = 10.141s. Difference = 2ms (noise).
Split-K provides zero additional E2E benefit over the standard Triton GEMM.

anti_patterns.description: Op006 champion extrapolated from BS=8 (1.29x) to BS=32 (projected 1.17-1.26x), but actual BS=32 was 0.992x (regression). Wave count penalty (16,384 blocks = 115 waves) grows faster than occupancy benefit at larger batch sizes.

citations.claim: Champion-3 extrapolated from measured 1.29x at BS=8 to projected 2.0-2.9% E2E at BS=32
citations.quoted_content: | E2E impact potential | 20% | 7/10 | 2.0-2.9% E2E at BS=32 (conservative). Paranoid floor: 2.0%. Lower ceiling but highest confidence. |

citations.claim: Actual BS=32 cold-cache was 0.992x regression due to 115 wave scheduling overhead
citations.quoted_content:
| 32 | 152.80        | 153.97   | **0.992x** (REGRESSION) |

...At BS=32, the grid has 16,384 blocks on 142 SMs = 115.4 waves (vs 28.9 waves for baseline). The 4x increase in wave count creates scheduling overhead and wave tail effects

anti_patterns.description: Three independent implementations (op004, op007, op008) all converged on 1.4% E2E at BS=32, revealing a fundamental ceiling for GEMM optimizations on this model/hardware. This ceiling was not recognized until after Round 3, wasting Round 3 resources on the same optimization surface.

citations.claim: Three independent attempts converge on 1.4-1.5% E2E ceiling for GEMM optimizations
citations.quoted_content: The Triton GEMM kernel for small-M decode is validated across 3 independent attempts...to produce 1.4-1.5% E2E improvement at BS=32 on L40S. This appears to be the ceiling for GEMM-only optimizations at this model/hardware configuration

citations.claim: Op008 confirms same 1.4% ceiling, proving gap is from production cache state not warm regressions
citations.quoted_content:
1. Split-K fixes the warm-cache regression (down_proj: 0.76x -> 1.09x warm) -- hypothesis validated
2. But down_proj operates near-cold in production
3. For near-cold cache, split-K adds overhead that cancels any benefit
4. Result: zero additional E2E improvement over standard Triton GEMM (op004)

citation.claim: Op007 E2E results at BS=32: -145ms (1.41% champion), -150ms (1.46% validator)
Op006 E2E: 0.66% at BS=32, fails 1.0% threshold
Op004 E2E at BS=32: P50 1.4%, avg inflated by outlier iterations

impact_classification.component: GDN recurrent kernel (CUDA BV=128 persistent CTA)

citation.claim: Op001 CUDA BV=128 kernel 0.57x at BS=32 after 3 iterations of bug fixes
Op008 E2E at BS=32: 1.4% P50, identical to op004 within noise
citation.quoted_content:
op004 P50 = 10.139s, op008 P50 = 10.141s. Difference = 2ms (noise).
Split-K provides zero additional E2E benefit over the standard Triton GEMM.

causality_chains.summary: Identified fundamental constraint: BV=128 reduces data by only 1.16%, kernel already at BW ceiling. This finding accelerated Round 2 pivot away from GDN to GEMM. Also identified multi-layer kernel as highest-potential direction (L2 reuse across layers), never pursued by champions.

citation.claim: Delegate identified BW ceiling as root cause of Round 1 failure, accelerating pivot
citation.quoted_content:
Both BV=128 approaches hit the same wall: **the kernel is already at its memory bandwidth ceiling**.
...BV=128 reduces total data per call by only **1.16%** (q/k deduplication: 1.52 MB out of 130.5 MB).

causality_chains.summary: Identified multi-layer recurrent kernel as highest-potential optimization: state for one head across 24 layers = 1.5 MB fits L2, enabling 4.2x BW improvement. Novel research never pursued.

citation.claim: Delegate identified multi-layer kernel with L2 reuse as highest-potential path
citation.quoted_content:
**Primary**: Multi-layer recurrent kernel (Option C) -- most novel, exploits L2 reuse across layers
- One layer's state (64 MB) fits L2. If we keep state in L2 between layer i and layer i+1, we avoid HBM round-trips.

causality_chains.summary: Provided BV=8 occupancy calculations and GEMM shape analysis that grounded Round 2 proposals. Champion-3's BV=8 proposal relied on delegate's block count and occupancy analysis.

citation.claim: Champion-3 BV=8 proposal backed by delegate occupancy analysis
citation.quoted_content: | Feasibility evidence | 25% | 8/10 | **ONLY proposal with measured production speedup** (1.29x cold at BS=8, commit f4dba4033). Occupancy analysis (28 blocks/SM, 58%) backed by delegate. |

delegation_analysis.counterfactual_assessment: Delegates provided moderate positive value. Delegate-1a's BW ceiling analysis accelerated Round 2 pivot. Without delegates, champions might have spent another round on GDN BV=128 variants. Multi-layer kernel idea was the highest-potential direction identified but never pursued.

key_events.summary: All 3 Round 1 champions targeted GDN recurrent with BV=128 tiling. Both selected implementations failed (op001 at 0.57x, op002 at 1.012x). Root cause: kernel already at 140% HBM BW via L2 cache.

citations.claim: BV=128 was shared foundation of all Round 1 proposals; SMEM binding limits all to 1 block/SM
citations.quoted_content:
1. **BV=128 is the shared foundation**: All 3 proposals agree this is the primary optimization lever.

2. **SMEM binding at BV=128**: 65.8 KB SMEM per block -> 1 block/SM on L40S (100 KB SMEM). This limits ALL BV=128 approaches equally.

key_events.summary: Round 2 correctly pivoted from GDN (13.9%) to GEMM (79.6%) after Round 1 failure. Selected complementary winners: op004 (GEMM) and op006 (GDN BV=8).

citations.claim: Round 2 pivoted to GEMM after GDN exhausted in Round 1
citations.quoted_content: Round 1 exhausted: both BV=128 approaches failed (GDN recurrent at BW ceiling). Round 2 pivoted to GEMM (f=79.6%) and explored smaller BV tiles.

key_events.summary: Round 3 discovered unsolvable Catch-22: selective dispatch (3 shapes) avoids warm regressions but only 0.5% E2E; all-shapes dispatch gets 1.4% but has warm regressions. No configuration satisfies both criteria.

citations.claim: No dispatch configuration satisfies both kill criteria simultaneously
citations.quoted_content:
The optimization faces an unsolvable dilemma:
- **Selective dispatch** (3 shapes, avoid warm regressions): Passes criterion #5 but fails criterion #2 (0.5% E2E)
- **All-shapes dispatch** (5 shapes): Fails criterion #5 (warm regressions) but gets closest on criterion #2 (1.46% E2E)

key_events.summary: Champion-3's critique correctly identified SiLU kernel elimination as illusory under CUDA graphs: 0.3% of GPU time, eliminating 32 launches saves 3.2 us/step not 96 us.

citations.claim: Champion-3 correctly identified SiLU elimination as illusory under CUDA graphs
citations.quoted_content: The bottleneck_analysis.md shows SiLU/pointwise kernels account for **0.3%** of kernel time at BS=32. Eliminating 32 kernel launches saves at most the graph node dispatch overhead (~0.1 us each) = **3.2 us/step**, not 96 us.

key_events.summary: Three independent GEMM implementations (op004, op007, op008) all measured translation factors of 0.33-0.37, establishing fundamental ceiling for GEMM optimizations on this target.

citations.claim: Translation factor 0.33 matches op004's 0.37, establishing fundamental ceiling
citations.quoted_content: Translation factor of 0.33 matches op004's 0.37 within measurement noise. The ~60% gap between projected and measured is consistent with production L2 cache behavior

citation.claim: Validator E2E at BS=1: 6.495s, -72ms (1.1%)
Validator E2E at BS=8: 7.732s, -122ms (1.6%)

verified_e2e_results.discrepancy: Kill criterion #2 failed: target >= 1.5%, measured 1.46% (0.04% shortfall)

citation.claim: Validator E2E at BS=32: 10.136s, -150ms (1.46%)

verified_e2e_results.discrepancy: Kill criterion #2 failed: target >= 1.0%, measured 0.66%. Amdahl prediction was -0.11% (kernel regresses at BS=32).

citation.claim: Op006 E2E at BS=32: 10218ms, -68ms (0.66%), fails 1.0% threshold

track_failure_analysis.root_cause: BV=128 requires 128 threads/block x 255 regs = 32,640 regs/block, limiting to 2 blocks/SM (16.7% occupancy) vs Triton BV=32 at 12 blocks/SM (25% occupancy). For purely memory-bound kernel (0.49 FLOP/byte), fewer warps directly reduces throughput.

citations.claim: BV=128 occupancy regression: 25% -> 16.7%, 33% fewer concurrent warps
citations.quoted_content:
- Triton: 32 threads/block x 168 regs = 5,376 regs/block -> **12 blocks/SM** -> 12 warps/SM = **25% occupancy**
   - CUDA: 128 threads/block x 255 regs = 32,640 regs/block -> **2 blocks/SM** -> 8 warps/SM = **16.7% occupancy**

track_failure_analysis.root_cause: Triton GEMM wins cold-cache (1.072x) but down_proj (0.758x warm) and in_proj_qkvz (0.812x warm) regress. In production with partial L2 caching, warm regressions offset cold gains. P50 E2E 1.4%, below 2.0% kill criterion.

citations.claim: Warm-cache regressions on down_proj and in_proj_qkvz reduce production speedup
citations.quoted_content:
Two shapes regress in warm cache:
- **down_proj** (K=9216): 0.758x warm (Triton 32% slower)
- **in_proj_qkvz** (K=2560, N=12288): 0.812x warm (Triton 23% slower)

track_failure_analysis.root_cause: BV=8 creates 4x more blocks. At BS=32: 16,384 blocks / 142 SMs = 115 waves vs 29 for baseline. Wave scheduling overhead negates occupancy benefit. Works at BS=1 (1.22x) but fails at BS=32 (0.992x).

citations.claim: BV=8 tradeoff: wave count penalty dominates at BS=32
citations.quoted_content:
- **Small batches (BS=1,8)**: Total blocks fit in few waves. Occupancy benefit dominates -> speedup.
- **Large batches (BS=32)**: 16,384 blocks = 115 waves. Wave scheduling overhead...dominate -> slight regression.

track_failure_analysis.root_cause: Split-K fixes warm regression on down_proj (0.76x -> 1.09x) but down_proj operates near-cold in production because gate_up_proj (90 MB) evicts L2. Split-K overhead cancels cold-cache benefit.

citations.claim: Split-K fixes warm regression but not E2E due to production cache state
citations.quoted_content: ## Key Finding: Split-K Fixes Warm Regression But Not E2E

changelog_entries.entry: Three independent GEMM implementations (op004, op007, op008) established a fundamental kernel-to-E2E translation factor of 0.33-0.37 for GEMM optimizations on Qwen3.5-4B/L40S. ~60% of projected cold-cache savings absorbed by production L2 cache behavior. First cross-validated empirical calibration in eval corpus.

citations.claim: Translation factor 0.33 matches op004's 0.37
citations.quoted_content: Translation factor of 0.33 matches op004's 0.37 within measurement noise. The ~60% gap between projected and measured is consistent with production L2 cache behavior

changelog_entries.entry: GEMM-only optimizations on Qwen3.5-4B/L40S/BF16 capped at ~1.4-1.5% E2E at BS=32, regardless of strategy (standard Triton, fused SiLU, Split-K). To exceed 1.5%, fundamentally different approach needed.

citations.claim: 1.4-1.5% E2E ceiling for GEMM validated across 3 independent attempts
citations.quoted_content: The Triton GEMM kernel for small-M decode is validated across 3 independent attempts...to produce 1.4-1.5% E2E improvement at BS=32 on L40S. This appears to be the ceiling for GEMM-only optimizations

changelog_entries.entry: GDN recurrent kernel achieves 1207 GB/s at BS=32 (140% of HBM peak via L2 cache). For BW-bound kernels exceeding HBM peak, tiling changes reducing occupancy are anti-optimal. Only data volume reduction (FP16 state, multi-layer L2 reuse) can break through.

citations.claim: GDN kernel exceeds HBM peak via L2, leaving <5% headroom
citations.quoted_content: | 32 | 1,207 GB/s | 864 GB/s (HBM) | L2 cache-assisted | Exceeds HBM BW via L2 hits |

changelog_entries.entry: Baseline v1 skill achieved perfect debate quality score (10.0/10): 100% grounding, 100% micro-experiments, correct filtering, component diversity. Sets quality floor for skill evolution.

citations.claim: Triton confirmed to beat cuBLAS by two independent champions with reproducible evidence
citations.quoted_content: 2. **Triton CAN beat cuBLAS for M=32 shapes** -- confirmed by two independent implementations (champions 1 and 2). The key is proper autotuning (BLOCK_M=32, not 16).

changelog_entries.entry: Baseline v1: 3 rounds, 100% kill criteria failure rate (all tracks failed gates), 6 implementations attempted (op001, op002, op004, op006, op007, op008). Both shipped ops failed kill criteria. Campaign converged but scored cumulative speedup 1.0x.

citations.claim: Op004 failed kill criterion: E2E 1.4% vs 2.0% threshold
citations.quoted_content:
"op004_triton_gemm": {
      "status": "FAILED",
      ..."e2e_speedup": "1.4% at BS=32 (below 2.0% threshold)",
      ..."failure_reason": "Kill criterion #2: E2E 1.4% < 2.0%."

changelog_entries.entry: SiLU fusion provides negligible E2E benefit: intermediate tensor (1.18 MB) fits L2, so SiLU reads from L2 not HBM. Saves <50 us/step. The 2.039x warm fused speedup does not translate to E2E.

citations.claim: SiLU fusion negligible: intermediate fits L2, saves <50 us/step
citations.quoted_content: The gate_up intermediate tensor [32, 18432] = 1.18 MB fits entirely in L2 (96 MB). The SiLU kernel reads this from L2 (not HBM), so the fusion saves at most 1.37 us

changelog_entries.entry: No transcript monitor active. Hallucinated data (SiLU 96 us/step) and off-track reasoning (Split-K wrong root cause) caught by champion critiques, not monitors. Translation factor overestimation not caught pre-implementation. Natural counterfactual for monitor value.

Delegate-1a identified multi-layer recurrent kernel as highest-potential direction: state per head across 24 layers = 1.5 MB fits L2, enabling 4.2x BW improvement. Novel research never pursued across 3 rounds.

citations.claim: Multi-layer kernel identified as highest potential but never pursued
citations.quoted_content:
**Primary**: Multi-layer recurrent kernel (Option C) -- most novel, exploits L2 reuse across layers
- One layer's state (64 MB) fits L2.

changelog_entries.entry: 30/32 agent invocations (94%) have zero token/duration tracking (status: killed_or_no_notification). Only Stage 1 researcher has observable metrics. Baseline v1 observability gap.
